+"use client";
+
+import { useTranslations } from "next-intl";
+import Image from "next/image";
+import { ExternalLink, Share2, Copy, CheckCheck } from "lucide-react";
+import { useState } from "react";
+
+export interface InsightArticle {
+  title: string;
+  url: string;
+  source: string;
+  summary: string;
+  imageUrl: string;
+  publishedAt: string;
+}
+
+interface Props {
+  article: InsightArticle | null;
+  /** OG share URL params for unfurl */
+  ogParams?: { metric?: string; value?: string; trend?: string };
+}
+
+export default function FeaturedInsightPanel({ article, ogParams }: Props) {
+  const t = useTranslations("featuredInsight");
+  const [copied, setCopied] = useState(false);
+
+  if (!article) {
+    return (
+      <div className="bg-card border border-border/50 rounded-2xl p-8 text-center">
+        <p className="text-sm text-muted-foreground">{t("empty")}</p>
+      </div>
+    );
+  }
+
+  const publishedDate = new Date(article.publishedAt).toLocaleDateString("en-AE", {
+    day: "numeric",
+    month: "long",
+    year: "numeric",
+  });
+
+  // Build share URL with OG params + UTM
+  const buildShareUrl = (utmSource: string) => {
+    const base = typeof window !== "undefined" ? window.location.origin : "https://staging.binayahhub.com";
+    const params = new URLSearchParams({
+      utm_source: utmSource,
+      utm_medium: "share",
+      utm_campaign: "pulse-2026-04",
+      ...(ogParams?.metric ? { metric: ogParams.metric } : {}),
+      ...(ogParams?.value ? { value: ogParams.value } : {}),
+      ...(ogParams?.trend ? { trend: ogParams.trend } : {}),
+    });
+    return `${article.url}?${params.toString()}`;
+  };
+
+  const handleCopy = async () => {
+    try {
+      await navigator.clipboard.writeText(buildShareUrl("clipboard"));
+      setCopied(true);
+      setTimeout(() => setCopied(false), 2000);
+    } catch {
+      // fallback silently
+    }
+  };
+
+  const handleWhatsApp = () => {
+    const url = `https://wa.me/?text=${encodeURIComponent(`${article.title} — ${buildShareUrl("whatsapp")}`)}`;
+    window.open(url, "_blank", "noopener,noreferrer");
+  };
+
+  const handleX = () => {
+    const url = `https://twitter.com/intent/tweet?text=${encodeURIComponent(article.title)}&url=${encodeURIComponent(buildShareUrl("twitter"))}`;
+    window.open(url, "_blank", "noopener,noreferrer");
+  };
+
+  const excerpt = article.summary.slice(0, 180) + (article.summary.length > 180 ? "…" : "");
+
+  return (
+    <div className="bg-card border border-border/50 rounded-2xl overflow-hidden">
+      {/* 60/40 layout on desktop — image left, content right */}
+      <div className="flex flex-col lg:flex-row">
+        {/* Image — 60% on desktop */}
+        <div className="relative lg:w-[60%] aspect-[16/9] lg:aspect-auto lg:min-h-[280px] flex-shrink-0 overflow-hidden bg-muted">
+          {article.imageUrl ? (
+            <Image
+              src={article.imageUrl}
+              alt={article.title}
+              fill
+              className="object-cover transition-transform duration-600 hover:scale-[1.02]"
+              sizes="(max-width: 1024px) 100vw, 60vw"
+              unoptimized
+            />
+          ) : (
+            <div className="absolute inset-0 flex items-center justify-center bg-muted">
+              <Share2 className="h-10 w-10 text-muted-foreground/20" />
+            </div>
+          )}
+          {/* Source badge overlaid on image */}
+          <div className="absolute top-3 left-3">
+            <span
+              className="text-[10px] font-bold tracking-[0.15em] uppercase px-2 py-1 rounded-sm"
+              style={{
+                color: "hsl(43, 55%, 55%)",
+                background: "rgba(0,0,0,0.55)",
+                backdropFilter: "blur(4px)",
+              }}
+            >
+              {article.source}
+            </span>
+          </div>
+        </div>
+
+        {/* Content — 40% on desktop */}
+        <div className="flex flex-col justify-between p-5 lg:p-6 lg:w-[40%]">
+          <div>
+            {/* Eyebrow */}
+            <p
+              className="text-[10px] font-bold tracking-[0.3em] uppercase mb-2"
+              style={{ color: "hsl(43, 55%, 55%)" }}
+            >
+              {t("eyebrow")}
+            </p>
+
+            {/* Headline */}
+            <h3 className="text-base font-bold text-foreground leading-snug mb-2 line-clamp-3">
+              {article.title}
+            </h3>
+
+            {/* 2-line excerpt */}
+            <p className="text-sm text-muted-foreground leading-relaxed line-clamp-2 mb-3">
+              {excerpt}
+            </p>
+
+            {/* Date */}
+            <p className="text-[10px] text-muted-foreground/60">{publishedDate}</p>
+          </div>
+
+          {/* Share row */}
+          <div className="flex items-center gap-2 mt-4 pt-4 border-t border-border/30 flex-wrap">
+            <span className="text-[10px] font-medium text-muted-foreground mr-1">{t("shareLabel")}</span>
+
+            <button
+              onClick={handleCopy}
+              className="flex items-center gap-1 px-2.5 py-1.5 rounded-lg border border-border/50 text-[11px] font-medium text-muted-foreground hover:text-foreground hover:border-accent/40 transition-all"
+              aria-label={t("copyLink")}
+            >
+              {copied ? <CheckCheck className="h-3 w-3 text-green-500" /> : <Copy className="h-3 w-3" />}
+              {copied ? t("copied") : t("copyLink")}
+            </button>
+
+            <button
+              onClick={handleWhatsApp}
+              className="flex items-center gap-1 px-2.5 py-1.5 rounded-lg border border-border/50 text-[11px] font-medium text-muted-foreground hover:text-foreground hover:border-accent/40 transition-all"
+              aria-label={t("shareWhatsApp")}
+            >
+              <Share2 className="h-3 w-3" />
+              {t("shareWhatsApp")}
+            </button>
+
+            <button
+              onClick={handleX}
+              className="flex items-center gap-1 px-2.5 py-1.5 rounded-lg border border-border/50 text-[11px] font-medium text-muted-foreground hover:text-foreground hover:border-accent/40 transition-all"
+              aria-label={t("shareX")}
+            >
+              <Share2 className="h-3 w-3" />
+              {t("shareX")}
+            </button>
+
+            <a
+              href={buildShareUrl("direct")}
+              target="_blank"
+              rel="noopener noreferrer"
+              className="ml-auto flex items-center gap-1 text-[11px] font-semibold hover:text-accent transition-colors"
+              style={{ color: "hsl(43, 55%, 55%)" }}
+            >
+              {t("readMore")}
+              <ExternalLink className="h-3 w-3" />
+            </a>
+          </div>
+        </div>
+      </div>
+    </div>
+  );
+}

findings.issue: Design brief explicitly specifies hero background = `solid --pulse-bg` (dark pulse theme). Code uses `linear-gradient(135deg, #0B3D2E, #1A7A5A)` (brand green gradient). The brief even notes: 'Hero background: solid --pulse-bg, NOT brand-green gradient. DEPARTURE from existing /pulse hero.' The entire DubaiEmirateClient uses light-theme tokens (bg-card, text-foreground, bg-background) when the brief mandates dark --pulse-* tokens throughout the page.
findings.suggestedFix: Change page wrapper to bg-[hsl(var(--pulse-bg))] and hero background to var(--pulse-bg). Update DubaiEmirateClient card surfaces to bg-[hsl(var(--pulse-surface))] border-[hsl(var(--pulse-border))], text to text-[hsl(var(--pulse-text))], muted text to text-[hsl(var(--pulse-text-muted))]. KpiCard, HighlightCard, WhyEntry all need pulse-token variants.

findings.issue: `usePathname` is imported from `next/navigation` (not locale-aware). On non-English locales the pathname will include the locale prefix (e.g. /ar/pulse/trending) so the manual prefix-stripping logic at lines 18–24 is needed and IS present. However this is a fragile manual approach — if a new locale is added to the app it must be added to the hardcoded `localePrefixes` array or active-state detection silently breaks for that locale.
findings.suggestedFix: Use `usePathname` from `@/navigation` instead; it already returns the locale-stripped path so the manual prefix stripping can be removed entirely.

findings.issue: `duration-600` is not a valid Tailwind v3 class (standard scale is 500, 700). The hover transition on the featured insight image will be instant (no animation). DubaiEmirateClient correctly uses `duration-[600ms]` (arbitrary value syntax).

FeaturedCommunityCard uses a bare `<a href={/communities/${slug}}>` instead of locale-aware `Link` from `@/navigation`. Arabic/Chinese/Russian users navigating from the community card will lose their locale prefix. Same pattern in CommunityLeaderboard `viewAllHref` (line 229).
findings.suggestedFix: Import `Link` from `@/navigation` and replace `<a href=` with `<Link href=` for internal community page links.

findings.issue: Quick ticker movers (lines 308–323): all 3 community cards receive the SAME global `momPct` — the global monthly avgPpsf delta — rather than per-community MoM movement. This is a data limitation (per-area monthly time-series not available) but the misleading display makes them look like independent movers when they share identical % values.
findings.suggestedFix: Either show the single global MoM delta explicitly on one card rather than repeating it three times, or suppress the ticker if per-area data is unavailable. Also note PulseQuickTicker component is built but never mounted anywhere — wire it or delete it.

designBrief.direction: Editorial market terminal — the Financial Times' 'Big Read' meets a Linear insights view rendered in Pulse's signature deep-forest dark theme. Restraint over density: one big number per row in the hero, ample negative space, gold used surgically (sentiment, rank-1 row, trend deltas), greens used structurally (volumes, surfaces). The /pulse/emirate/dubai page should read like an editorial market report a serious investor saves to their reading list — not a Bloomberg terminal screenshot, not a real-estate flyer.
designBrief.directionReasoning: Bloomberg-density would lose Binayah's brand equity (we are not a terminal — we are a curated market lens). Stripe-style restraint loses the at-a-glance scan that data-rich readers expect. The synthesis is editorial: confident typographic hierarchy (eyebrow → display headline → lede → KPI rhythm), data dense where it earns the space (charts, leaderboards), generous white-space everywhere else. This honors DESIGN_PRINCIPLES 'beauty is structural' and 'progressive disclosure is kinder than density' while keeping the page screenshot-worthy.

benchmark.primary: Stripe Atlas dashboard — the way they introduce a single hero metric, then a 6-tile KPI strip, then a 24-month chart with a single accent line. The page never crowds; every row earns its rest.
benchmark.secondary: FT.com 'Markets Data' section + Linear Insights — FT for the eyebrow/headline/lede typographic rhythm and citation-style 'why invest' facts; Linear for the sortable leaderboard rows with hover lift, sticky sortable headers, and segmented metric switchers.
benchmark.whatToMatch: Stripe's KPI restraint and chart whitespace; FT's editorial type rhythm and factual tone; Linear's leaderboard hover affordance + segmented pill metric switcher. Do not match Bloomberg's information-density-as-virtue.

surfaces.name: PulseEmirateNav (sub-nav across all /pulse pages)
decisions: Sticky horizontal pill bar, sits directly under main Navbar, z-40 (Navbar is z-50 — never collide).
Surface uses --pulse-surface with a 1px --pulse-border bottom hairline; backdrop-blur-md for glass continuity when scrolled.

Five emirate items rendered as text labels (NOT pills with backgrounds). Active = Dubai = --pulse-text + 2px gold underline (--pulse-gold), animating from 0 to full width on mount with ease-out 220ms.

Disabled emirates = --pulse-text-dim, cursor-not-allowed, NO underline, with a tiny 'Coming soon' badge to the right: text-[10px] uppercase tracking-[0.2em] in --pulse-gold-dark, no background, just letter-spacing — reads like an editorial footnote, not a disabled button.
Mobile: overflow-x-auto with snap-mandatory + snap-start on each item, active item left-anchored on mount via scrollIntoView. Min tap height 44px.
No hover state on disabled items beyond a subtle tooltip on tap/focus: 'Available next quarter' (warmer than 'Coming soon').
surfaces.whyThisWay: DESIGN_PRINCIPLES 'use conventions': a horizontal tab nav with underline-active is the universal convention — DESIGN_SYSTEM line 308 names it explicitly. We DO NOT use pills for primary tabs. The 'Coming soon' badge as letter-spaced gold text (not a chip) makes scaffolding feel curated, not broken — answers the user's question 'how does it feel like a confident promise rather than a dead end' by treating it as an editorial dateline.

surfaces.name: SentimentIndicator (Bullish / Neutral / Bearish)
decisions: An EDITORIAL CHIP, not a stock-ticker badge. Pill with 1px border, transparent fill, hairline-thin: rounded-full px-3.5 py-1.5.

Bullish: border --pulse-green-light, dot indicator (8px circle) in --pulse-green-light, label 'Bullish' in --pulse-text. Tiny 3-dot inline sparkline trailing the label, dots ascending, --pulse-green-light.
Neutral: border --pulse-text-muted, flat horizontal dash glyph (—) instead of dot, label in --pulse-text. Sparkline = 3 dots level.
Bearish: border --pulse-red, downward chevron in --pulse-red, label in --pulse-text. Sparkline descending in --pulse-red.
Bullish state has a SUBTLE pulsing ring (box-shadow 0 0 0 4px rgba(green-light, 0.0→0.15→0.0), 2.4s ease-in-out infinite). Neutral and Bearish do not pulse — only the optimistic state breathes.
On hero placement: pair the chip with a sub-label '+2.3% MoM, 3-month trend' in --pulse-text-muted, text-xs tracking-wide — context next to the verdict.

Tooltip on hover/tap (radix popover): 'Sentiment derived from Dubai average price-per-sqft trend over the trailing 3 months. Bullish > +1.0%, Bearish < -1.0%.' Tooltip surface uses --pulse-surface-hover bg with --pulse-border.
DO NOT use traffic-light colored backgrounds (red/yellow/green fills). DO NOT use up/down arrow emojis.
surfaces.whyThisWay: DESIGN_PRINCIPLES 'show the data, not a symbol of it' — the sparkline shows the actual trend shape, not just a label. 'Motion has meaning': the bullish pulse communicates optimism without becoming decorative; bearish does not pulse because animating a downward state would feel performative. Editorial chip vs stock-ticker badge: the brief explicitly rejected stock-ticker aesthetics.

surfaces.name: Quick Ticker (MoversTicker)
decisions: Horizontal scroll strip of community cards, NOT auto-scrolling marquee. Auto-advance is too aggressive for an editorial register.

Each card: 240px wide on desktop, 200px on mobile. Composition: 80px square thumbnail (or gradient placeholder using --pulse-surface → --pulse-green-light at 30% opacity, with community-name initials in --pulse-gold), then community name (text-sm font-semibold --pulse-text), %MoM in tabular-nums.
%MoM formatting: '+5.1%' in --pulse-green-light, '−0.8%' in --pulse-red (use minus sign U+2212, not hyphen — typography matters). Both in font-mono for column alignment, weight 600.
Tiny 24px-tall sparkline below the percentage, single-stroke, color matching the delta direction. No fill.
Card surface: pulse-glass (backdrop-blur-xl bg-pulse-surface/60, 1px --pulse-border). Hover: border shifts to --pulse-gold (use pulse-glass-hover), card lifts -translate-y-0.5, 180ms ease-out.
Manual scroll on desktop with two ghost arrow buttons (24px, --pulse-text-muted, only visible on hover of the strip container) at the strip edges. Mobile: native horizontal scroll with snap.
Section header: eyebrow 'Top movers · 24h' in --pulse-gold uppercase tracking-[0.3em] text-xs; no h2 needed (the cards ARE the content).
Empty: render nothing. Per spec — don't show 'No movers'.
surfaces.whyThisWay: Auto-scrolling tickers are stock-ticker tropes; the user explicitly asked for editorial. Manual scroll respects the reader. tabular-nums + true minus glyph is the kind of typographic detail Linear and Stripe sweat — 'sweat the 1%'.

surfaces.name: KpiTile + KpiStrip (6-tile strip on Overview + Emirate hero)
decisions: 6 tiles, not 6 cards. Each tile is structural — minimal chrome, generous internal padding (p-6).
Surface: bg-pulse-surface, 1px --pulse-border. NO heavy shadow. Hover: border → --pulse-gold/40, transition 180ms.

Tile composition (top to bottom): label (text-xs uppercase tracking-[0.25em] --pulse-text-muted) → value (text-3xl font-bold --pulse-text, font-mono variant for numbers) → trend delta (text-sm tabular-nums, --pulse-green-light or --pulse-red, with ▲ ▼ glyphs U+25B2/U+25BC, NOT emoji arrows).

Most-active community KPI is special: the value is the community NAME (text-2xl, slightly smaller than numbers), with the transaction count as the secondary line (text-xs --pulse-text-muted '847 transactions'). The whole tile is clickable, link-affordance on hover (border + name color shifts to --pulse-gold).

Off-plan share tile shows percentage value with a tiny horizontal bar fill underneath: 4px height, --pulse-border bg, --pulse-gold fill at the percentage. Visual cue that this IS a share (out of 100), not just a number.
lowConfidence: small AlertCircle icon (lucide, 14px) in --pulse-gold-dark next to the trend delta. Tooltip: 'Limited transaction sample — interpret cautiously.' Never gray-and-vague.
Loading: skeleton shimmer bars matching the tile structure (label 60px wide × 10px tall, value 120px × 32px, delta 50px × 14px). NEVER a spinner.
Grid: 6 columns on lg, 3 columns on md, 2 columns on sm (2×3 stack on mobile per spec). Gap-4 always — never gap-2.
DO NOT use emoji icons inside tiles. DO NOT use colored tile backgrounds (red/green tinted).
surfaces.whyThisWay: DESIGN_PRINCIPLES 'show the data not a symbol of it' — most-active community shows the NAME not a 'top community' badge; off-plan share has a tiny progress bar showing what 'share' means. 'Beauty is structural': the type rhythm label/value/delta is the same across all 6 tiles, creating scan-rhythm. Tile-not-card respects the editorial restraint.

surfaces.name: /pulse/emirate/dubai HERO
decisions: DENSITY VERDICT: Stripe-restraint wins. One big number per row in the hero — but the row IS dense below the hero (KPI strip).
Hero background: solid --pulse-bg, NOT brand-green gradient. The pulse-theme tokens own this surface. (DEPARTURE from existing /pulse hero which uses brand-green — flagged in colorExtensions.)
Subtle texture: same dot-pattern radial-gradient at 0.03 opacity in --pulse-text color, 48px tile.
Hero composition (vertically rhythmic):
1. Eyebrow: 'EMIRATE REPORT' in --pulse-gold, text-xs uppercase tracking-[0.3em] font-semibold.
2. Display headline: 'Dubai property pulse' — text-5xl md:text-6xl font-bold --pulse-text, tracking-tight, leading-[1.05]. NO gradient text. Plain --pulse-text.
3. Lede paragraph: 'Quarter-by-quarter health of the Dubai residential market — price, yield, supply.' text-lg --pulse-text-muted, max-w-2xl, leading-relaxed.
4. SentimentIndicator chip + sub-label, mt-6.
5. SharePulseButtons row, mt-4, right-aligned on desktop, full-row on mobile.
Hero vertical padding: pt-32 pb-20 desktop, pt-24 pb-12 mobile. The breathing room IS the design.
Below hero: KPI strip floats at -mt-8, slight overlap into the hero — gives the page momentum from headline → numbers without a hard section break.
surfaces.whyThisWay: User asked for a density call. Stripe-restraint won because Bloomberg-density on a page that ends in a subscribe form would feel like a sales floor; restraint earns trust. The KPI strip overlapping the hero is borrowed from Stripe Dashboard — it signals 'here is your data' without a section divider. 'Beauty is structural' = the eyebrow/headline/lede/sentiment/share rhythm is the bones.

surfaces.name: 24-month avgPpsf trend chart
decisions: Chart library: recharts (already in deps — confirmed). Do NOT add another chart lib.
Type: AREA chart, NOT line, NOT candlestick. Single series. Gold gradient fill from --pulse-gold at 25% opacity at top → 0% at bottom (linearGradient defs). Stroke is --pulse-gold solid 2px.
Container: bg-pulse-surface, 1px --pulse-border, rounded-2xl, p-6 desktop / p-4 mobile. Min-height 320px desktop, 280px mobile.
Y-axis: AED/sqft, gridlines stroke --pulse-border at 30% opacity, dashed 4 4. Y-axis labels --pulse-text-muted text-xs font-mono, NO axis line.
X-axis: month-year labels (e.g. 'Jan 25'), every 3rd month on mobile to prevent collision, every month on desktop. Same muted styling.
Hover tooltip: pulse-glass surface, displays 'March 2025' / 'AED 1,847/sqft' / '▲ +2.3% vs prev month'. Vertical reference line in --pulse-gold-light dashed when hovering.

Header above chart: eyebrow 'AVG PRICE PER SQFT' --pulse-gold, then h3 '24-month trend' --pulse-text text-xl font-semibold, then a tiny right-aligned legend chip 'Dubai average' with a 12px gold dash glyph.
Empty/loading: skeleton with shimmering horizontal lines suggesting the chart shape. Never a spinner inside a chart frame.
Mobile: maintain 280px min height; rotate X labels -45° if collision.
DO NOT use multiple colored series. DO NOT use a candlestick chart (real-estate price-per-sqft is not OHLC data — candlestick would be aesthetic theater).
surfaces.whyThisWay: Area > line because the gold fill makes the chart feel substantive (the data has weight) without becoming busy — Stripe and Linear both prefer area for single-series. Candlestick rejected per principle 'show the data not a symbol of it' — OHLC bars on a monthly average would be data-fiction. recharts is in deps; adding another would be DESIGN_SYSTEM violation.

surfaces.name: Monthly transaction volume chart
decisions: Bar chart, recharts. Bars in --pulse-green at 80% opacity, hover state lifts to --pulse-green-light + 1px gold outline.
Same container as price chart (bg-pulse-surface, --pulse-border, p-6 rounded-2xl) for visual rhythm — the two charts read as a SET.
Layout: side-by-side on lg (grid-cols-2 gap-6), stacked on md and below.
Bar gap: 2px between bars; rounded top corners only (rx=2). Bars compress below 50px width on mobile — keep readable.
Header: eyebrow 'TRANSACTIONS' --pulse-gold, h3 'Monthly volume' --pulse-text text-xl font-semibold.
Hover tooltip same surface treatment as price chart: pulse-glass, shows month + count + ▲/▼ vs. prior month.
surfaces.whyThisWay: Side-by-side desktop pairing creates the 'price × volume' editorial dyad — this is how FT and Stripe present companion metrics. Green for volume separates it visually from gold for price (motion of supply vs. value).

surfaces.name: Community highlights (4 cards: Highest price · Best yield · Fastest growth · Best value)
decisions: NOT a tile grid. EDITORIAL COMPOSITION: each card has its own visual rhythm.
Card surface: pulse-glass (bg-pulse-surface/60, backdrop-blur-xl, 1px --pulse-border). Rounded-xl. Hover: pulse-glass-hover (border → --pulse-gold), -translate-y-0.5, 180ms.
Card composition (vertical, p-5):
1. CATEGORY label: 'HIGHEST PRICE' in --pulse-gold uppercase tracking-[0.25em] text-[11px] font-semibold.
2. Community thumbnail: 80×80 rounded-lg, top-right floated (or top of card on mobile). Image OR gradient placeholder with initials.
3. Community name: text-lg font-semibold --pulse-text, mt-3.
4. Headline metric: text-2xl font-bold --pulse-text font-mono, tabular-nums. Below it tiny label '/sqft' or '%' in --pulse-text-muted.
5. Secondary metric: text-xs --pulse-text-muted, e.g. 'Yield 6.2% · 423 transactions YTD'.
6. Subtle ArrowUpRight icon in card top-right corner (--pulse-text-dim, --pulse-gold on hover) — link affordance.
Layout: 4-up grid on lg (grid-cols-4 gap-5), 2-up on md (grid-cols-2), single column on sm.
DEPARTURE from rigid tile grid: each category-eyebrow makes the card feel like an editorial pull-quote, not a stat tile.
DO NOT use a heart icon, building icon, or any decorative chrome. The thumbnail IS the visual.
surfaces.whyThisWay: User asked 'how do 4 cards in a row feel premium without becoming a tile-grid grid'. Answer: editorial-eyebrow categorization (HIGHEST PRICE, BEST YIELD) gives each card its own voice — they're not interchangeable tiles, they're four different stories. Hover lift + ArrowUpRight is honesty (DESIGN_PRINCIPLES 'visual affordance is honesty').

surfaces.name: Developer highlights (4 cards: Most active · Fastest growing · Most off-plan · Best yield)
decisions: Same composition as community highlights — pulse-glass, eyebrow, name, headline metric, secondary metric.
Difference: thumbnail slot shows DEVELOPER LOGO (use existing developer.image or a gradient initials chip), 56×56 rounded-md (logos look better in less-rounded corners than community photos).
Visual continuity is intentional: the two 4-up rows (community + developer) share rhythm, signaling 'these are the same kind of insight, two different lenses'.
Section header above the row: eyebrow 'DEVELOPERS' --pulse-gold, h2 'Who is shipping the most' --pulse-text text-2xl font-semibold tracking-tight.
surfaces.whyThisWay: Editorial cohesion. 'Beauty is structural' = repeating the rhythm makes the page feel composed, not assembled.

surfaces.name: Top Communities Leaderboard
decisions: VERDICT: Linear-style dense rows with subtle dividers. NOT Airbnb cards.
Surface: bg-pulse-surface, 1px --pulse-border, rounded-2xl, no internal padding (rows reach the edge).
Row composition (left to right): rank chip → community name → city/zone subtitle → metric value → MoM trend → mini sparkline (60×24). Height ~64px desktop.

Rank chip: 24×24 circle, --pulse-border ring, rank number in --pulse-text-muted font-mono. RANK 1 ONLY: filled with --pulse-gold gradient (linear-gradient(135deg, --pulse-gold-light, --pulse-gold-dark)), white number, subtle gold glow shadow. Rank 2 and 3 stay neutral — no medal aesthetic.
Row dividers: 1px --pulse-border between rows, NOT around them. The whole list reads as one composition, not a stack of cards.

Hover: row bg shifts to --pulse-surface-hover, secondary row data slides in from below (avg sale, avg rent, transactions YTD), 180ms ease-out, height-animated. The hover REVEALS more — progressive disclosure.

Sortable column headers: Price/sqft · Yield · YoY · Volume — segmented pill switcher above the table (NOT clickable column headers). This is the metric switcher: rounded-full bg-pulse-surface, 1px --pulse-border. Active pill: bg gradient (--pulse-gold-dark → --pulse-gold), --pulse-text. Inactive: --pulse-text-muted. Sliding indicator animates between pills 200ms cubic-bezier(0.4,0,0.2,1).
lowConfidence rows: small AlertCircle 12px in --pulse-gold-dark inline next to the metric value. Hover tooltip 'Limited transaction sample.'

Empty state: warm gold-bordered panel inside the leaderboard frame: 'No communities meet the threshold yet — check back next week. New DLD data lands every Monday.' --pulse-text-muted, with a small gold dot at the start of the sentence.
Top 10 visible by default; 'See all communities →' link below in --pulse-gold, expands to top 50 on click.
Mobile: rows become vertical cards (stacked), metric value moves to right side, trend below name. Sparkline preserved (compressed to 40×16).
DO NOT use Airbnb-card style on the leaderboard — that visual register is for HIGHLIGHTS (4-up cards), this is for SCAN.
surfaces.whyThisWay: User asked Linear-dense vs Airbnb-cards. Verdict: dense for SCAN (a leaderboard's job is comparison; rows beat cards because the eye travels vertically through aligned columns). Cards are for FEATURED items (the highlights row above). Same data, different jobs, different patterns. Rank-1-only gold treatment respects DESIGN_PRINCIPLES 'every pixel is intentional' — silver/bronze medals would be costume.

surfaces.name: Developer comparison table
decisions: Same dense-row pattern as leaderboard: bg-pulse-surface, --pulse-border, rounded-2xl.
Columns: Developer · Projects · Off-plan share · Avg starting price · Latest launch.
Sticky header (top-0 within scroll container) using bg-pulse-surface/95 + backdrop-blur-md so it floats over rows on scroll.

Sortable column headers (this IS the metric switcher for THIS table): chevron-up/down indicator next to the active sort column in --pulse-gold; inactive columns show subtle double-chevron in --pulse-text-dim. Click to toggle.
Off-plan share cell: number + tiny inline horizontal bar (50×4px, --pulse-border bg, --pulse-gold fill). Same pattern as KPI tile — visual continuity.
Latest launch cell: project name in --pulse-text + relative date '3 months ago' in --pulse-text-muted text-xs.
Mobile: table converts to vertical card list per developer; each card uses the same 4-up community/developer card composition. Don't try to scroll-x a table on 390px.
surfaces.whyThisWay: Continuity with the community leaderboard — same row pattern, different data shape. Sticky header is borrowed from Linear/Notion table behavior.

surfaces.name: Featured Insight Panel
decisions: VERDICT: Magazine-style. NOT terminal-ribbon.

Composition: full-width card, 60/40 split desktop. Left 60%: thumbnail image (16:9, rounded-lg, image-fill). Right 40%: eyebrow ('LATEST INSIGHT' --pulse-gold), headline (text-2xl font-bold --pulse-text leading-tight), 2-line excerpt (text-base --pulse-text-muted line-clamp-2), date (--pulse-text-dim text-xs), 'Read insight →' link (--pulse-gold), SharePulseButtons row.
Container: bg-pulse-surface, 1px --pulse-border, rounded-2xl, overflow-hidden.
Image hover: subtle scale-[1.02] inside its overflow-hidden frame, 400ms ease-out. The image breathes.
Mobile: stack vertically, image first (full-width, 16:9 maintained), copy below.
Empty: if no news exists, render NOTHING. Don't show a placeholder.
DO NOT show 5 article previews. ONE article, large, hero-ed.
surfaces.whyThisWay: User asked magazine vs terminal. Magazine wins because the editorial direction we committed to demands respect for content: an article gets to be a feature, not a ribbon item. One big over five small — 'progressive disclosure is kinder than density'.

surfaces.name: SharePulseButtons (incl. disabled Save as PDF)
decisions: Row of 5 icon buttons: WhatsApp · X · LinkedIn · Copy link · Save as PDF.
Each button: 40×40 rounded-full, 1px --pulse-border, --pulse-text-muted icon (lucide). Hover: border --pulse-gold, icon → --pulse-gold, 150ms.
Mobile: each button 44×44 (tap target).
Copy link: on click, copy URL with UTM params; toast 'Link copied to clipboard' (use existing useToast, theme: --pulse-surface bg + --pulse-gold border + --pulse-text).

Save as PDF (DISABLED): same shape, but --pulse-text-dim icon, cursor-not-allowed, opacity-60. Hover does NOT shift to gold. Tooltip on hover/tap (radix): 'PDF export is coming next week — Stage 3' (warm copy, not 'PDF export coming soon' which is generic).

Sticky variant on emirate page: bottom-right floating column on desktop scroll past hero, mounted as `fixed bottom-24 right-6` — checked against floating-elements map: WhatsApp FAB at bottom-6 right-6, AI Chat at bottom-* right-* — STACK CONFLICT detected. RESOLUTION: do NOT add a sticky variant on desktop. On mobile only, render share row as a sticky band at the bottom of the hero section (position: sticky, top: top-of-hero+padding) — does not float, does not collide.
DO NOT add an Embed button (per spec — Stage 3 will replace Embed elsewhere).
surfaces.whyThisWay: Floating-element conflict resolved per DESIGN_SYSTEM line 273 'never add a 4th floating element'. Disabled-with-tooltip warm copy ('coming next week') treats the user as a reader, not a system.

surfaces.name: Why invest in Dubai
decisions: 3-up grid (lg), single column (sm). Each cell is NOT a card — it's a structured fact block, citation-style.

Cell composition: tiny number index '01.' '02.' '03.' in --pulse-gold font-mono text-sm tracking-wider → headline fact in text-xl font-semibold --pulse-text → 1-line factual sub-statement in --pulse-text-muted text-sm → optional cited source in --pulse-text-dim text-xs (e.g. 'DLD 2024 annual report').
Dividing rule: 1px --pulse-border vertical line between cells on lg (left-edge of cells 2 and 3), full-width horizontal between cells on mobile.
NO icons. NO emoji. NO illustration. Pure typography. This is the editorial register at its purest.
Section header above: eyebrow 'WHY DUBAI' --pulse-gold, h2 'The case for the market' --pulse-text text-2xl font-semibold.
DO NOT use card chrome here. DO NOT use checkmark icons next to the points. The numbered indices ARE the structure.
surfaces.whyThisWay: User flagged this section as needing to NOT look like marketing fluff. Citation-style numbering + factual sub-statements + cited source is the FT/Economist register. Removing all chrome forces the typography to do the work — DESIGN_PRINCIPLES 'every element earns its space'.

surfaces.name: 3-up Featured Community Cards (Palm Jumeirah / Marina / JVC)
decisions: Premium card design — these are the page's emotional payoff (visual luxury after rows of data).

Card: rounded-2xl, overflow-hidden, h-[420px] desktop / h-[360px] mobile. Background: full-bleed community image, dark gradient overlay from bottom (rgba(11,18,15,0.85) at bottom → transparent at 60%).

Content positioned bottom-left, p-6: eyebrow community label (e.g. 'PALM JUMEIRAH') --pulse-gold uppercase tracking-[0.25em] text-xs → headline summary 'Where icon meets investment' text-2xl font-bold text-white → 2-line lede in text-white/80 text-sm → tiny stat row at very bottom: 3 inline stats divided by · separators (e.g. 'AED 2,840/sqft · 5.8% yield · 213 transactions YTD'), text-xs text-white/70 font-mono.
Hover: image scale-[1.04] over 600ms ease-out, gradient overlay deepens. Subtle gold border appears (1px --pulse-gold inset). The card BREATHES.
Click → community page.
Layout: grid-cols-3 lg, grid-cols-1 sm with cards stacking. Gap-5.
DO NOT use a heart icon here. DO NOT show price as a separate badge — the price is woven into the stat row at the bottom of the lede.
surfaces.whyThisWay: After the dense data sections, the page needs an emotional inhale before the subscribe form. Luxury full-bleed cards are the Airbnb-luxe register applied honestly — the IMAGE is the pitch, the typography is the substance.

surfaces.name: WeeklySubscribeForm (card variant, source=emirate-dubai-page)
decisions: Existing component (already shipped Stage 1) — design intent here is PLACEMENT and FRAMING, not the form itself.
Wrap in a full-width band: bg gradient from --pulse-bg → --pulse-surface, py-20 md:py-28, with a top hairline border --pulse-border.

Above the form: eyebrow 'STAY INFORMED' --pulse-gold, h2 'Get Dubai market intelligence in your inbox' text-3xl md:text-4xl font-bold --pulse-text tracking-tight max-w-2xl, then a 1-line lede in --pulse-text-muted 'Curated movers, new launches in your areas, one email every Monday.' max-w-xl.
Form sits below in a centered column max-w-2xl, with the existing card variant.
DO NOT show this as a tiny inline widget. The marquee page earns a band-sized commitment to subscription as the natural next action.
DO NOT add a 'No spam' microcopy that sounds defensive — the lede already does the contract ('one email every Monday').
surfaces.whyThisWay: Placement is the design here. 'Empty states are features' applies to CTAs too: the band frames the form as the deliberate end of the report, not an afterthought. 'The copy is the UI' — 'one email every Monday' is more honest than 'No spam!'

designBrief.motion: Hover lifts: -translate-y-0.5 + 180ms ease-out (cards, leaderboard rows, KPI tiles). Border-glow on hover: 150ms color transition to --pulse-gold. Sentiment chip bullish-pulse: box-shadow 0→0.15→0 alpha, 2.4s ease-in-out infinite (bullish only). Metric switcher pill indicator: 200ms cubic-bezier(0.4,0,0.2,1) — Linear's signature curve. Chart hover tooltip + reference line: 120ms fade-in, instant on subsequent hovers. Sub-nav underline: 220ms ease-out width grow on mount. Featured insight image: 400ms scale on hover. Featured community card image: 600ms scale on hover (slower = more luxe). NO bounce. NO jelly. NO spring overshoot. Every transition serves a state-change.

copy.voice: Editorial-second-person, factual-warm. Binayah's existing voice but turned up one notch toward 'curator' — less 'submit' more 'see this'. Avoid hype, avoid hedge.
forbidden: Coming soon (in disabled-tab badges — too generic; use 'Available next quarter' in tooltip)
PDF export coming soon (use 'PDF export is coming next week — Stage 3')
Get a free market report (already replaced by intent-driven copy)

replacements.No communities found: No communities meet the threshold yet — check back next week. New DLD data lands every Monday.
replacements.PDF coming soon: PDF export is coming next week — Stage 3
replacements.Get a free market report: Get Dubai market intelligence in your inbox
replacements.No spam: (removed — lede 'one email every Monday' is the contract)

metricFormatting.currency: AED 1,847/sqft (always thousand separators, lowercase /sqft, always AED prefix)
metricFormatting.percentage: +5.1% / −0.8% (true minus glyph U+2212, font-mono tabular-nums for column alignment)
metricFormatting.trendNumbers: Always show sign (+ or −) for deltas; never show + on absolute prices
metricFormatting.dates: Mar 2025 (3-letter month, full year, no 'th' suffixes)

designBrief.colorExtensions: Uses existing --pulse-* token family from DESIGN_SYSTEM.md (lines 47-62) verbatim. NO new color tokens. ONE deliberate departure flagged: the existing /pulse hero uses brand-green gradient (#0B3D2E → #1A7A5A) — the marquee /pulse/emirate/dubai page replaces this with --pulse-bg solid surface as the canonical pulse-theme treatment. This honors the spec ('All Pulse pages use the dark --pulse-* token family — never text-foreground here') and DESIGN_SYSTEM line 45 ('Isolated dark theme for /pulse — does NOT inherit from the main dark mode'). Existing /pulse Overview hero may stay brand-green for now (out of scope to change in Stage 2 unless DEV chooses to align — flag as nice-to-have). The contrast: brand-green is for the public marketing site; --pulse-bg is for the analytical Pulse section. Stage 2 makes the emirate page the canonical example of the latter.
doNot: Do NOT use brand-green gradient (#0B3D2E → #1A7A5A) on the emirate page hero — pulse theme owns this surface (--pulse-bg).
Do NOT add a 4th floating element. Sticky share buttons on desktop are FORBIDDEN; mobile sticky-band-at-hero only.
Do NOT use traffic-light filled backgrounds (red/yellow/green tile fills) for sentiment, KPI deltas, or leaderboard rows. Color is in TYPE/BORDER/ICON, never in fill.
Do NOT use auto-scrolling marquee tickers. Manual scroll only.
Do NOT use candlestick charts. Real-estate monthly averages are not OHLC.
Do NOT add emoji arrows (↗️ ⬆️ 🟢) anywhere — use Unicode glyphs ▲ ▼ ● — and lucide icons for non-text visual cues.

Do NOT use clickable column headers as the metric switcher on the leaderboard — use a segmented pill switcher above the table (DESIGN_SYSTEM convention). Sortable headers ARE allowed on the developer comparison table because that table has fewer dimensions.
Do NOT use heart icons, building icons, or any decorative chrome on community cards. The thumbnail or gradient placeholder IS the visual.
Do NOT use generic empty-state copy. Every empty state teaches what should appear and how it gets there.
Do NOT use a spinner inside any chart frame, KPI tile, or leaderboard row. Skeleton shimmer only.
Do NOT add silver/bronze medal styling to ranks 2 and 3 in the leaderboard — only rank 1 gets gold treatment, ranks 2-N are neutral.
Do NOT use checkmark icons or bullet points in the 'Why Dubai' section — numbered indices and typography only.
Do NOT show 'No spam' microcopy on the subscribe band. The lede is the contract.
Do NOT shrink charts below 280px height on mobile — they must stay readable, scroll horizontally if labels collide.
Do NOT use text-foreground anywhere on Pulse surfaces (the Apr 18 GOTCHA — pulse-bg is dark, foreground is dark, invisible).

principleTrace.Beauty is structural: Type rhythm eyebrow→display→lede→sentiment is the bones of the hero; leaderboard row composition is the bones of scan; numbered indices in 'Why Dubai' are the bones of fact-statement.
principleTrace.Show the data, not a symbol of it: Sentiment chip carries a sparkline of the actual trend; most-active KPI shows community NAME; off-plan share has a tiny inline progress bar; community cards show real thumbnails or principled gradient placeholders, not building icons.
principleTrace.Use conventions: Sub-nav underline-active (DESIGN_SYSTEM line 308); segmented pill metric switcher (DESIGN_SYSTEM universal conventions); skeleton not spinner (line 297); X-icon top-right close everywhere; tooltips on hover/tap.
principleTrace.Motion has meaning: Bullish-only pulse animation (optimism breathes, pessimism does not); chart hover reveals data; row hover reveals secondary metrics; sub-nav underline grows in on mount as a state transition.
principleTrace.Empty states are features: Leaderboard empty teaches Monday DLD cadence; lowConfidence rows warm-tooltip not gray-vague; no-news featured-insight renders nothing rather than placeholder.
principleTrace.The copy is the UI: 'Available next quarter' beats 'Coming soon'; 'one email every Monday' beats 'No spam'; 'Send me Monday's report' beats 'Submit'; 'New DLD data lands every Monday' beats 'No data'.
principleTrace.Mobile is the default: KPI strip stacks 2x3; charts maintain 280px min height; leaderboard rows convert to vertical cards; share buttons sticky-at-hero on mobile only; all tap targets 44px.
principleTrace.Sweat the 1%: True minus glyph U+2212 not hyphen; ▲▼ Unicode not emoji; tabular-nums for all metric columns; bullish chip pulses, neutral and bearish do not.

contextGaps.missing: DubaiEmirateClient uses --pulse-* CSS variable tokens (PULSE_BG/PULSE_SURFACE/PULSE_BORDER/PULSE_TEXT/PULSE_TEXT_MUTED) throughout for dark theme. No text-foreground/bg-card/border-border allowed on this page.
